title: 10 best herbal essential oils for hypertension
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for hypertension are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the symptoms of high blood pressure by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving cardiovascular health. The best herbal essential oils for hypertension include Valeriana officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Ginkgo biloba, Melissa officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Rosmarinus officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, Panax ginseng, Rauvolfia serpentina, and Astragalus membranaceus. If left untreated, hypertension can lead to heart disease, stroke, and kidney damage, severely impacting daily life, causing fatigue, shortness of breath, and increased risk of premature death.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn essential oils helps with hypertension because they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to dilate blood vessels, reduce blood pressure, and improve circulation. The oil's flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins help to relax the cardiovascular system, reducing stress on the heart and decreasing peripheral resistance. This natural remedy has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to support cardiovascular health, and its essential oil form provides a convenient and effective way to promote blood pressure management.
remedy_properties: Cardioprotective compounds: Hawthorn essential oil contains flavonoids and procyanidins, which have been shown to help protect the heart from damage and reduce blood pressure by improving cardiovascular function.
Vasodilation: The beta-sitosterol content in Hawthorn essential oil helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing resistance to blood flow and lowering blood pressure.

Antioxidant activity: Hawthorn essential oil's high antioxidant capacity helps to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage to cardiovascular tissues, further supporting its cardioprotective effects.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, hawthorn roots have been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular diseases, making them a valuable component in essential oils for hypertension.

Barks: The barks of hawthorn trees contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including proanthocyanidins and oligomeric procyanidins, which can help lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest crataegus monogyna leaves at dawn on sunny days for optimal potency retention.
Dry the fresh leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill dried crataegus monogyna leaves using a clevenger apparatus with water at 100°c.
Collect and filter the essential oil within 30 minutes of distillation, yielding approximately 2% crude oil.
Transfer the crude oil to dark glass bottles, store in a cool place, and allow it to mature for 14 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with hypertension because it has a natural calming effect on the body, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that often contribute to high blood pressure. The oil's ability to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality also plays a crucial role in managing hypertension, as both stress and lack of sleep are known triggers for elevated blood pressure. Additionally, lavender essential oils have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, further contributing to its beneficial effects on blood pressure regulation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Lavender essential oil's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which are known to contribute to hypertension development and progression.
Calcium channel blocker: The terpenoid compounds present in lavender oil have been shown to exhibit calcium channel-blocking activity, which may help relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure.

Anxiolytic and sedative: Lavender essential oil's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative properties can help reduce stress levels, promote relaxation, and mitigate the effects of hypertension-related anxiety, ultimately contributing to improved blood pressure regulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are used to create a calming and sedative essential oil, which helps to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.

Seeds: The seeds of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of compounds that help to lower blood pressure and improve circulation, making them an effective part for creating medicinal essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in early morning for optimal oil quality.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers thoroughly to prevent contamination and mold growth.
Steam distill 100g of the clean dried flowers at atmospheric pressure using 500ml of distilled water for 1 hour.
Collect the essential oil produced during steam distillation, filtering it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the resulting lavandula angustifolia essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label as "best used by" 6 months from production date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with hypertension because they contain antioxidants that help to reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common contributors to hypertension. Additionally, rosemary has been shown to improve the body's response to insulin, reducing blood sugar levels and further contributing to its blood pressure-lowering effects. Overall, incorporating rosemary essential oils into your wellness routine may be a natural way to support healthy blood pressure management.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant property helps combat oxidative stress and inflammation, which are believed to contribute to the development of hypertension.
Vasodilator: Rosemary essential oil has been shown to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure by allowing for easier blood flow and improving circulation.
Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to hypertension and its complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary contain a high concentration of camphor, a compound that helps to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
Buds: Rosemary buds are rich in ursolic acid, a natural compound that has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which can help to lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated for best potency.
Wash the harvested plant material gently with clean water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry with paper towels.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems with 300 milliliters of distilled water at 60°c for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle to remove any impurities.
Store the extracted rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with hypertension because it contains a unique compound called sesquiterpene lactone, which has been shown to effectively relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure. The oil also possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the constriction of blood vessels caused by inflammation, further contributing to its hypotensive effects. Regular use of maidenhair tree essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of hypertension and promote a healthier cardiovascular system.
remedy_properties: Antihypertensive property: Maidenhair Tree essential oil has been shown to have antihypertensive effects, which helps to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of Maidenhair Tree essential oil help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of hypertension.

Cardiovascular protecting property: Maidenhair Tree essential oil has been found to have cardiovascular-protecting effects by reducing lipid peroxidation, improving blood vessel function, and preventing platelet aggregation, all of which can help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease associated with hypertension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, roots are believed to help reduce blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
Barks: Containing phenolic compounds, barks have been shown to exhibit anti-hypertensive properties by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves at dawn or late afternoon for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, dark place for at least 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine one cup of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 14-21 days, shaking the jar every 3-4 days to ensure even extraction.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids and transferring the essential oil to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with hypertension because of its ability to regulate blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The oil's potent antioxidants and flavonoids help to relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and increasing blood flow. Additionally, geranium oil's natural sedative properties can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common contributing factors to high blood pressure. By promoting a healthy balance of bodily functions, geranium essential oils can be an effective natural remedy for hypertension sufferers.

Blood pressure regulator: The oil's ability to regulate blood pressure is attributed to its effect on the body's sympathetic nervous system, which helps to balance blood pressure levels.

Inflammation reducer: Geranium essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and cardiovascular system, which can contribute to hypertension.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the geranium plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to help lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Geranium rhyzomes have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat hypertension due to their ability to calm the nervous system and regulate blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and stems at dawn when they are most fragrant and potent.
Wash the harvested plant material with distilled water and gently pat it dry with a paper towel.
Steam distill the dried pelargonium graveolens leaves and stems in a stainless steel still for 4 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles containing 5% of vitamin e oil as an antioxidant.
Store the herbal pelargonium graveolens essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for at least 2 weeks to allow the flavors and aromas to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with hypertension because it has been shown to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure. The oil contains a compound called thymol, which has a positive effect on the cardiovascular system by increasing nitric oxide production, leading to vasodilation and reduced resistance in blood flow. Additionally, thyme essential oil's antispasmodic properties help to reduce muscle tension in blood vessel walls, further contributing to its hypotensive effects. By promoting relaxation and improving blood flow, thyme essential oils can provide a natural remedy for managing hypertension.
remedy_properties: Antihypertensive: Thyme essential oil has been shown to have a direct relaxing effect on blood vessels, which can help lower blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and cardiovascular system, which is often associated with high blood pressure.

Cardiovascular-protective: The antioxidants present in thyme essential oil can help protect the heart and cardiovascular system from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of hypertension and other heart-related conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used to make essential oil due to their ability to calm and relax the nervous system, which helps in reducing blood pressure and alleviating hypertension symptoms.

Buds: Thyme buds contain high amounts of flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help in reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, making them an effective component for medicinal essential oils for hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from healthy plants grown organically without pesticides.
Dry the thyme leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Steam distill the dried thyme leaves with water at a ratio of 20:1 using a still for 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the distilled mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Store the thymus vulgaris essential oil in an airtight dark container away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with hypertension because of its ability to calm the nervous system and reduce stress levels. The oil contains a compound called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to help lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow. Additionally, sage oil's calming properties can help to reduce anxiety and stress, both of which are common triggers for high blood pressure. Calcium channel blocking activity: The essential oil's calcium channel-blocking property helps to relax blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and improve cardiovascular function by inhibiting the entry of calcium ions into cardiac cells.

Antihypertensive properties: Sage essential oil has been shown to possess antihypertensive effects through its ability to reduce blood pressure, possibly due to its flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which may help to relax smooth muscle and improve blood vessel function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing blood pressure and hypertension.
Barks: Sage bark contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to lower blood pressure by dilating blood vessels and improving blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia sclarea leaves at dawn when dew is still present on the leaves.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days until crisp and brittle.
Steam distill 100g of dried salvia sclarea leaves with 500ml of water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separator funnel and filter paper.
Store the resulting salvia sclarea essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep refrigerated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with hypertension because of its unique ability to relax the body's vascular system, reducing blood pressure and promoting a sense of calm. The oil's sedative properties help to slow down the heart rate and promote better circulation, which can lead to a significant decrease in blood pressure. Additionally, valerian oil has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can also contribute to its ability to alleviate hypertension symptoms. Calcium channel blocker effects: Valerian oil has been shown to inhibit the activity of calcium channels in smooth muscle cells, leading to relaxation of blood vessels and a decrease in blood pressure.

Anxiolytic properties: Valerian oil's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to high blood pressure, by promoting relaxation and reducing sympathetic nervous system activity.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots at dawn for maximum potency and quality.
Clean and chop the harvested roots into small pieces, ensuring minimal waste and uniform size.
Steam distill the chopped root pieces using 10 kilograms of water per kilogram of plant material over three hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coarse filter paper to remove impurities and sediment.
Bottle the pure valeriana officinalis essential oil in dark glass containers, storing it at room temperature for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with hypertension because it has natural blood vessels widening properties, allowing for improved circulation and reduced pressure on the cardiovascular system. The anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger also help to relax the smooth muscle lining of blood vessels, further aiding in reducing blood pressure. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to high blood pressure. By incorporating ginger essential oils into one's daily routine, individuals may find relief from hypertension symptoms and improved overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to high blood pressure.

Vasodilatory Effects: The active compound 6-gingerol in ginger essential oil helps to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure by counteracting the effects of vasoconstriction.

Cardioprotective Compounds: Ginger essential oil contains cardio-protective compounds like sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce cardiac arrhythmias, improve myocardial function, and lower blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant are rich in volatile compounds and have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory effects, making them useful for managing blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger rhizomes and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the washed ginger rhizomes in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least 7 days.
Grind the dried ginger into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for about 5 minutes.
Steam distill 500g of the ground ginger with 1 liter of water for 2 hours to produce essential oil.
Filter the resulting mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the essential oil from the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with hypertension because it contains cineole, a compound that has been shown to have a positive effect on blood pressure. Studies have demonstrated that cineole can help to dilate blood vessels and reduce blood vessel resistance, leading to a decrease in systolic blood pressure. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties may also play a role in reducing inflammation in the body, which is often associated with hypertension. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eucalyptus oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to high blood pressure.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Tasmanian Blue Gum oil help protect against oxidative stress and damage to the cardiovascular system, reducing the risk of hypertension-related complications.
Cardiovascular-protective properties: Eucalyptus oil's ability to dilate blood vessels and reduce blood pressure can help alleviate symptoms of hypertension, such as headaches and dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to help regulate blood pressure and reduce hypertension.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant contain triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of eucalyptus globulus at dawn when they are most aromatic and rich in oils.
Steam distill the leaves in a still with water at a ratio of 1 part leaves to 3 parts water for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the steam condensate using a separating funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its clarity after 15 minutes.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool dry place for up to 1 year.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hypertension with herbal essential oils is to combine Frankincense and Bergamot oils in a diffuser or apply topically after diluting with a carrier oil. Frankincense's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce blood pressure, while Bergamot's citrus scent promotes relaxation and stress relief, both of which contribute to hypertension control. Regular use can lead to sustained blood pressure reduction.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hypertension that are treated with herbal essential oils are anxiety, stress, and circulatory disorders. Essential oils like bergamot, lavender, and ylang-ylang have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of anxiety. Other herbs like ginkgo biloba and hawthorn help improve circulation and overall cardiovascular health, promoting healthy blood flow and lowering the risk of hypertension-related complications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with hypertension because of its adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to respond to stress more effectively. The oil contains valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting a sense of calm. By lowering cortisol levels and promoting a healthy stress response, Valeriana officinalis essential oils can help regulate blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms. This results in a more balanced cardiovascular system.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It has a vasodilatory effect, which helps in relaxing blood vessels and reducing blood pressure.
Valeranone: It acts as a calcium channel blocker, inhibiting the contraction of blood vessels and thus, helping to lower blood pressure.
Linalool: It has anxiolytic and sedative properties, which can help in reducing stress and anxiety-related hypertension.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are commonly used to make essential oils for hypertension due to their high concentration of valerenic acid and valepotriates, which have a sedative and calming effect.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Valeriana officinalis are used to make essential oils for hypertension because they contain a high amount of valepotriates, which help to reduce blood pressure and promote relaxation.

Leaves: The leaves of Valeriana officinalis are also used to make essential oils for hypertension due to their content of valerenic acid and other volatile compounds that help to reduce anxiety and stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of dried valeriana officinalis roots in the early morning to capture optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested roots at 50-70°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separator or condenser.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper or cloth filter to remove impurities and particles.
Store the final essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with hypertension because of its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress, two key factors contributing to high blood pressure. The oils' calming effects help slow down heart rate and promote vasodilation, allowing blood vessels to expand and improve blood flow. Additionally, the oils' sedative properties help regulate sleep patterns, which is often disrupted in individuals with hypertension. By addressing these underlying issues, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help alleviate hypertension symptoms and promote overall cardiovascular well-being.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: It helps with hypertension by acting as a vasodilator, which means it widens blood vessels and reduces blood pressure.
Linalyl acetate: It contributes to lowering hypertension by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key component in the regulation of blood pressure.
Caryophyllene oxide: It has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the underlying inflammation associated with hypertension.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their calming and antihypertensive properties.
Flowers: Rich in linalool, linalyl acetate, and other volatile compounds, they help to reduce blood pressure and promote relaxation.
Stems: They contain a significant amount of linalool and other essential oils that contribute to the plant's antihypertensive and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 kg of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny day in late morning.
Steam distill the flowers in a still with a ratio of 1:5 flowers to water.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water within 2 hours.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 2 weeks before use to develop its full aroma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with hypertension because it contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have vasodilatory properties. These compounds help to widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure. The anticoagulant properties of ginkgo biloba also prevent blood platelets from clumping together, which can contribute to high blood pressure. Additionally, ginkgo biloba has been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, promoting overall cardiovascular health and helping to regulate blood pressure. Regular use may help alleviate hypertension symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with hypertension by improving blood vessel function, reducing inflammation, and enhancing the body's ability to regulate blood pressure.
Bilobalide: It helps by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and lowering blood pressure.

Ginkgolides: These constituents help by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a substance that can increase blood pressure and promote blood clotting, thus contributing to better blood vessel function and lower blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most widely used part of Ginkgo biloba in essential oil production due to their high concentration of ginkgo flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help manage hypertension.
Seeds: The seeds are used to produce ginkgo biloba oil due to their high content of ginkgolides, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are used to produce essential oils with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate hypertension symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 40-50c for 2-3 hours to remove moisture content.
Steam distill the dried leaves for 4-6 hours to extract essential oil from the leaves.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by decanting it into a separate container.
Store the extracted ginkgo biloba essential oil in airtight glass bottles in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with hypertension because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. Linalool: As a vasodilator, linalool can help to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can contribute to lowering blood pressure.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hypertension.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, which has a significant impact on lowering blood pressure.
Flowers: They are used for their calming and relaxing properties, which can help in managing stress-related hypertension.
Stems: They are used as they contain a significant amount of essential oils, including camphor and borneol, which have vasodilatory effects and can help in reducing blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning when they are at their highest oil content.
Steam distill the plant material with 1 part melissa to 3 parts water at 100°f for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its fragrance and properties.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool dry place for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils helps with hypertension because it contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels, which are often associated with high blood pressure. Rotundine: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect blood vessels from damage and reduce blood pressure.

Fruits: They are used because they contain passifloric acid and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their potential antihypertensive effects.

remedy_recipe: Harvest passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves at peak bloom in late summer for optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a warm dark place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves at a temperature of 100-110 degrees fahrenheit for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel within 24 hours.
Store the passiflora incarnata essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with hypertension because of its vasodilatory properties, which allow blood vessels to relax and widen. remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: It helps with hypertension by acting as a vasodilator, causing blood vessels to relax and improve blood flow.
Bornel: It helps with hypertension by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which is involved in blood pressure regulation.
Bornyl acetate: It helps with hypertension by having a vasodilatory effect, which leads to a reduction in blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Rosmarinus officinalis for essential oil production due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which is believed to help reduce blood pressure.
Flowers: Flowers are also widely used for essential oil production as they contain a high amount of camphor and borneol, which may help lower blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms.

Stems: Stems are occasionally used for essential oil production, as they contain a smaller amount of rosmarinic acid compared to leaves and flowers but still contribute to the overall medicinal properties of the oil.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 40g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100°c for 2 hours using a steam distillation apparatus.
Collect the distillate and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities present.
Store the extracted rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from heat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils helps with hypertension because of its vasodilatory properties, which help to relax and widen blood vessels, reducing blood pressure. The oil's flavonoid content, particularly hypericin and hyperforin, contribute to its ability to improve blood flow and lower systolic and diastolic blood pressure. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum essential oil has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to high blood pressure. This results in a decrease in blood pressure and improved overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It helps with hypertension by reducing the activity of the sympathetic nervous system, which in turn lowers blood pressure.
Kaempferol: It helps with hypertension by acting as an antioxidant, preventing the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.
Quercetin: It helps with hypertension by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which is involved in the regulation of blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high concentration of naphthoquinones, which have been shown to have antihypertensive properties.
Leaves: Utilized for their flavonoid content, which helps to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Stems: Employed for their rich content of hyperforin, a compound that has been found to exhibit vasodilatory effects, thereby helping to lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh or 50 grams of dried flowers when they are in full bloom.
Dry the flowers in a warm dark place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Use a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin to extract the essential oil from the dried flowers.
Use a distillation apparatus to separate the essential oil from the solvent and other impurities.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a cotton cloth to remove any remaining impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng essential oils helps with hypertension because of its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress, a major contributor to high blood pressure. The oil's ability to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation also plays a crucial role in reducing blood pressure. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and contribute to hypertension. By promoting overall cardiovascular health, Panax ginseng essential oils provide a natural solution for managing hypertension. This can lead to improved heart health and reduced risk of related complications.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help with hypertension by exerting vasodilatory effects, relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow, which in turn lowers blood pressure.

Terpenoids (specifically, panaxynol and oleanolic acid): These compounds help with hypertension by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which plays a crucial role in blood pressure regulation, and also by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels.

Sesquiterpenes (specifically, cis- and trans-panaxydrol): These compounds help with hypertension by exhibiting vasodilatory effects, similar to ginsenosides, and also by modulating the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS), a key regulatory pathway for blood pressure control.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of ginsenosides, the active compounds responsible for the medicinal properties of Panax ginseng.
Stems: They contain a higher concentration of sesquiterpenes, which contribute to the oil's therapeutic effects on hypertension.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to have a positive impact on blood pressure regulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of panax ginseng roots at the optimal time when the plants are in full maturity.
Clean the roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities from the surface.
Steam distill the roots at 100-150c for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation technique.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for 6-12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina essential oils helps with hypertension because of its unique properties that promote relaxation and reduce blood pressure. The oil contains alkaloids like reserpine, which have been shown to inhibit the release of catecholamines, such as adrenaline, that constrict blood vessels and increase heart rate. By reducing the constriction of blood vessels and promoting vasodilation, Rauvolfia serpentina essential oils help to lower blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms, providing a natural and effective solution for those seeking to manage their blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Reserpine: It helps with hypertension by inhibiting the reuptake of norepinephrine, a neurotransmitter that raises blood pressure, thereby reducing sympathetic nervous system activity.
Yohimbine: It helps with hypertension by blocking alpha-2 adrenergic receptors, which in turn reduces the release of norepinephrine and decreases peripheral vascular resistance.
Alstonine: It helps with hypertension by causing vasodilation and reducing cardiac output, leading to a decrease in blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain alkaloids like reserpine, which helps lower blood pressure.
Leaves: They are a good source of reserpine and other alkaloids, contributing to their use in hypertension treatment.
Stems: The stems of Rauvolfia serpentina are also used to make essential oils that help in managing high blood pressure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus essential oils helps with hypertension because of its unique properties that promote cardiovascular health. The oil contains flavonoids and saponins that help to relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving circulation. It also exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to protect the blood vessels from damage and promote overall cardiovascular well-being. Additionally, the oil has been shown to help regulate blood flow and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, making it a potential natural remedy for hypertension management.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help reduce hypertension by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can lower blood pressure and prevent cardiovascular damage.
Saponins: Help alleviate hypertension by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key component in the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system that regulates blood pressure.
Flavonoids: Help combat hypertension by acting as vasodilators, improving blood flow and reducing vascular resistance, which can lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds that help in reducing blood pressure.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that contribute to cardiovascular health and blood pressure regulation.
Rhyzomes: Storehouses of bioactive compounds that help in modulating blood pressure and improving cardiovascular function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh astragalus membranaceus roots in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at 35-40 degrees celsius for 2-3 days.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and fill a 10-liter still with 6 liters of water.
Steam distill the root mixture for 4-6 hours to produce a light yellow essential oil.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and debris.
